title: While Loop
description: Repeat code as long as a condition is true.

slides.title: Why Loops? 🔁
slides.content:
Loops can execute a block of code as long as a specified condition is reached.

They are handy because they save time, reduce errors, and make code more readable. Instead of writing `cout << "Hello";` ten times, you can just loop it!

slides.title: The while Loop
slides.content:
The `while` loop loops through a block of code as long as a specified condition is `true`.

Here's an example that prints numbers from 0 to 4:

quizData.question: What does the code i++; do in the loop above?

options.text: It increases the number by 1 so the loop eventually ends

quizData.explanation: If we don't increase the variable, the condition (i < 5) will ALWAYS be true, creating an infinite loop.

slides.title: Infinite Loops ♾️
slides.content:
If you forget to increase (or decrease) the variable used in the condition, the loop will never end! This will crash your program or freeze the user's computer.

Always ensure the loop condition will eventually evaluate to `false`.

quizData.question: A while loop checks the condition BEFORE it executes the code block.
quizData.explanation: Yes, if the condition is false to begin with, the code block will NEVER be executed.
